ingredients.name: Kamurio Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge near Nanakita Park sits at the heart of the Sendai Colony, the real-world territory that became the battlefield for the Culling Game's first major arc in Season 3. Fans trace the bridge's distinctive silhouette to key transition shots during the Sendai Colony episodes.

ingredients.name: Inokashira Park
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-18-31 Gotenyama, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0005, Japan

ingredients.name: Nanakita Park Tennis Courts
ingredients.address: Nanakita Park Tennis Courts, Gakeshita-60-19 Nanakita, Izumi Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 981-3131, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Located inside Nanakita Park, these courts are set within the Sendai Colony barrier zone, the epicenter of Yuta Okkotsu's brutal Culling Game battles in Season 3. Pilgrim fans noted that the park's open grounds appear in the wide-angle battle overhead shots showing the colony's devastation.

ingredients.name: Sendai City Museum
ingredients.address: Sendai City Museum, 26 Kawauchi, Aoba Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 980-0862, Japan

ingredients.address: Japan, 〒981-3125 Miyagi, Sendai, Izumi Ward, Mizuhodai, 3-13 ズーズーカンパニー八乙女店

ingredients.name: SAKAE Izumi Chuo Building
ingredients.entityContext: This commercial building in the Izumi Chuo hub appears in the Sendai Colony arc as one of the urban landmarks composing the backdrop of the 5-6 km radius barrier Kenjaku erected around Sendai, trapping players inside for the Culling Game.

ingredients.name: HUMOS SWiNG
ingredients.entityContext: The station building attached to Izumi Chuo Station is one of the most recognizable structures in the Sendai Colony, a location shown repeatedly as Yuta Okkotsu fights through the barrier-enclosed city during the Culling Game arc in Season 3.

ingredients.name: SELVA Terrace
ingredients.address: SELVA Terrace, 1 Chome-６-3 Izumichuo, Izumi Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 981-3133, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This shopping complex adjacent to Izumi Chuo Station is part of the dense commercial district that forms the Sendai Colony battlefield in Season 3. The area's streets and storefronts are faithfully reproduced in the wide-angle shots of the chaotic Culling Game skirmishes.

ingredients.name: Komeda's Coffee Shinjuku Yasukuni-dori

ingredients.address: マトリクス・ツービル １F, 1 Chome-24-6 Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shibuya streetscape around this convenience store appears in the opening animation of Season 3, establishing the gritty urban atmosphere of Tokyo as the stage for the Culling Game.

ingredients.name: Courtyard by Marriott Tokyo Ginza Hotel
ingredients.localName: コートヤード・マリオット銀座東武ホテル
ingredients.address: Courtyard by Marriott Tokyo Ginza Hotel, 6 Chome-14-10 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0061, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This hotel in Ginza is identified by pilgrimage researchers as the rooftop where Yuta Okkotsu is spotted standing in Episode 1, with Yuji Itadori looking up at him from the street below in a tense early meeting that sets up their dynamic in the Culling Game.

ingredients.name: Ginza 1 Post Office
ingredients.address: Ginza 1 Post Office, 1 Chome-20-14 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0061, Japan

ingredients.name: Tsukudao Bridge
ingredients.address: Tsukudao Bridge, 大橋 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0044, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Tsukuda Bridge and its surrounding riverside in Chuo City appear in Episode 1 as part of the Ginza-area scenes where Yuji pursues a curse. Pilgrimage researchers matched the twin tower skyline of St. Luke's Garden visible from the bridge to the anime's background art.

ingredients.name: Kukyo Hall
ingredients.address: Kukyo Hall, Kuzakai Hall, 1, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 162-0823, Japan

ingredients.name: Myth of Tomorrow
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒150-0043 Tokyo, Shibuya, Dogenzaka, 1 Chome−12−1 渋谷マークシティ, ２階連絡通路
ingredients.entityContext: Taro Okamoto's massive atomic-bomb mural in the Shibuya Mark City walkway appears in the opening sequence of Season 3, its imagery of human struggle against destruction serving as a visual metaphor for the sorcerers trapped in the Culling Game. The mural is one of the most iconic free-to-visit pilgrim spots in all of Jujutsu Kaisen.

ingredients.name: House of Nishikawa Annex
ingredients.address: House of Nishikawa Annex, 3 Chome-7-1 桜町 Koganei, Tokyo 184-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Taisho-era Western-style building, preserved at the Edo-Tokyo Open Air Architectural Museum in Koganei, is confirmed as the visual model for the Zenin clan estate exterior in Episode 1. The building's distinctive architecture was reproduced closely in the scenes depicting the powerful Zenin family home.

ingredients.name: Yoyogi Park Art Studio
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒151-0063 Tokyo, Shibuya, Tomigaya, 1 Chome−6−14 Yoyogi Park Art Studio
ingredients.entityContext: The distinctive triangular art-mural building near Yoyogi Park appears in the opening animation of Season 3, its vivid facade used to evoke the chaotic creative energy of Tokyo as the backdrop for the Culling Game. Multiple pilgrimage guides identify this as an OP background location.

ingredients.name: Kodakara-yu
ingredients.address: Kodakara-yu, 2 Chome-9 Sekinocho, Koganei, Tokyo 184-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This beautifully preserved Showa-era public bathhouse at the Edo-Tokyo Open Air Architectural Museum is confirmed as the visual model for a building featured in the Season 3 opening, adjacent to the Zenin estate scenes. Its iconic curved gabled roof and ornate wooden exterior are faithfully reproduced in the anime's art direction.

ingredients.name: Kabuki-za
ingredients.address: Kabuki-za, 4 Chome-12-15 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0061, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo's iconic Kabuki-za theater in Ginza provides the surrounding streetscape that appears in Episode 1, where the distinctive building roofline and the dense block layout around Higashi-Ginza are reproduced in the anime's background art as Yuji moves through the city.

ingredients.name: Tonarie Tsukuba Square
ingredients.localName: トナリエつくばスクエア
ingredients.address: Tonarie Tsukuba Square, 1 Chome-6-1 Azuma, Tsukuba, Ibaraki 305-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Omiya Rakuun Yoshimoto Theater
ingredients.localName: 大宮ラクーンよしもと劇場
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒330-0802 Saitama, Omiya Ward, Miyacho, 1 Chome−６０ Omiya Rakuun Yoshimoto Theater, 大宮RAKUUN６Ｆ
ingredients.entityContext: This Saitama comedy venue is the real-world model for the theater where Takaba Fumihiko performs his stand-up routines in Episode 3. The building name in the anime mirrors RAKUUN, and the interior layout matches scenes showing Takaba's desperate attempts to make his cursed-spirit audience laugh.

ingredients.name: Kamurio Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge near Nanakita Park sits at the heart of the Sendai Colony, the real-world territory that became the battlefield for the Culling Game's first major arc in Season 3. Fans trace the bridge's distinctive silhouette to key transition shots during the Sendai Colony episodes.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Metropolitan Theatre
ingredients.address: Tokyo Metropolitan Theatre, 1 Chome-8-1 Nishiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This landmark theater in Ikebukuro anchors the Tokyo Colony No. 1 zone in Season 3 Episode 7, with the district between Ikebukuro and Toshima forming the boundary of the barrier. The building's recognizable facade is reproduced in establishing shots of the Tokyo Colony battleground.

ingredients.name: Nakameguro Atlas Tower
ingredients.localName: 中目黒アトラスタワー
ingredients.address: Nakameguro Atlas Tower, 1 Chome-26-1 Kamimeguro, Meguro City, Tokyo 153-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Pilgrimage researchers identified this high-rise condominium in Nakameguro as the rooftop building where one of the sorcerers stands in an overhead shot during Episode 7, with its distinctive profile matching the anime background art.

ingredients.name: Masamido Ceremony Hall
ingredients.localName: 正美堂セレモニーホール

ingredients.address: Nakano South Stella, 2 Chome-24-3 Nakano, Nakano City, Tokyo 164-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Iwamori Books
ingredients.address: Iwamori Books, 5 Chome-30-12 Ogikubo, Suginami City, Tokyo 167-0051, Japan

ingredients.name: Morioka District Court
ingredients.address: Morioka District Court, 9-1 Uchimaru, Morioka, Iwate 020-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Morioka District Court is the real-world model for the courthouse that appears in Higuruma Hiromi's backstory in Episode 8. The episode depicts his fateful defense case in Morioka, where he successfully argued for acquittal only to see it overturned, a defeat that drove him into the Culling Game. Pilgrimage fans walk along the nearby Nakatsu River to trace the exact route shown in the anime.

ingredients.address: Bishamon Bridge, Kamihoronai, Omu, Mombetsu District, Hokkaido 098-1708, Japan

ingredients.address: Lawson Morioka Kado, 1 Chome-18-40 Kado, Morioka, Iwate 020-0823, Japan

ingredients.name: Nishi-Ikebukuro Park
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-8-26 Nishiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small park directly outside Tokyo Metropolitan Theatre in Ikebukuro appears in Episode 8 as part of the Tokyo Colony No. 1 setting, located within the barrier zone where Yuji, Megumi, and Reggie clash in the Culling Game.

ingredients.name: Suwarochen Nakanoten
ingredients.localName: スワローチェーン 中野店
ingredients.address: Suwarochen Nakanoten, ＳＮクロスビル 3 Chome-33-20 Nakano, Nakano City, Tokyo 164-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Rikkyo University Post Office
ingredients.address: Rikkyō University Post Office, 5 Chome-10-14 Nishiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Located on the Rikkyo University campus in Ikebukuro, this post office is one of the background details faithfully reproduced in Episode 10 as Megumi Fushiguro and Reggie Star battle across the streets and grounds of the Tokyo Colony No. 1.

ingredients.name: Rikkyo University
ingredients.address: Rikkyo University, 3 Chome-34-1 Nishiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-8501, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The redbrick campus of Rikkyo University in Ikebukuro is featured in Episode 10 as a key location in the Tokyo Colony No. 1 arc, with its distinctive Gothic architecture used as background for the chase and battle between Megumi Fushiguro and Reggie Star. The campus buildings and tree-lined paths are closely matched to the anime art.

ingredients.name: Rikkyo Gakuin All Saints Chapel
ingredients.localName: 立教学院諸聖徒礼拝堂
ingredients.address: Rikkyo Gakuin All Saints Chapel, 3 Chome-34-1 Nishiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-8501, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This historic chapel on the Rikkyo University campus in Ikebukuro appears in Episode 10 alongside the broader campus scenery. Its red-brick exterior and Gothic detailing are reproduced in the anime's backgrounds during the intense Megumi vs. Reggie fight through Tokyo Colony No. 1.

ingredients.name: Kaneyamaen Bessho yada (Bessho Sasa)
ingredients.localName: 鐘山苑 別墅然然（べっしょ ささ）
ingredients.address: Kaneyamaen Bessho yada (Bessho Sasa), 9-chome-1-18 Kamiyoshidahigashi, Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi 403-0032, Japan

ingredients.name: Yahatagawa South Greenway
ingredients.address: Yahatagawa South Greenway, 4 Chome-5-5 Nishiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This peaceful tree-lined promenade near Ikebukuro appears in Episode 11 as part of the Tokyo Colony No. 1 zone, offering one of the quieter streetscape shots in the arc before the final confrontations of the Culling Game's first phase.

ingredients.name: Yurtec Stadium Sendai
ingredients.localName: ユアテックスタジアム仙台
ingredients.address: Yurtec Stadium Sendai, 柳-78 Nanakita, Izumi Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 981-3131, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yurtec Stadium is the real-world model for the football stadium that serves as the centerpiece of the Sendai Colony. In the Culling Game arc, Yorozu awaits Sukuna's arrival here at this landmark, and the stadium's immediate surroundings including Nanakita Park became the epicenter of fierce Culling Game battles. Official Jujutsu Kaisen commemorative manhole covers were installed in the surrounding park.

ingredients.name: Sendaishi Izumi Library
ingredients.address: Sendaishi Izumi Library, 1 Chome-8-6 Izumichuo, Izumi Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 981-3133, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Izumi Library sits within walking distance of Izumi Chuo Station, at the heart of the Sendai Colony barrier zone established by Kenjaku in Season 3. The library is part of the dense Izumi Chuo district whose streetscape is faithfully recreated in the Culling Game battle scenes.
